steps.title: Identify the density formula and what we need to find
steps.content: Density is mass divided by volume. We know the mass (8.10 grams) and the cube's edge length (3.00 centimeters), so we must first calculate the volume.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of the cube
steps.content:
For a cube with edge length 3.00 cm:
$$(3.00)^3 = 27.00 \text{ cm}^3$$

steps.title: Calculate the density
steps.content:
Divide the mass by the volume:
$$\frac{8.10 \text{ g}}{27.00 \text{ cm}^3} = 0.30 \text{ g/cm}^3$$
